title: Potentilla tabernaemontani longifrons
taxonomy: Potentilla tabernaemontani longifrons is a rare subspecies of the Potentilla tabernaemontani species. This subspecies belongs to the family Rosaceae. More specifically, it falls under the genus Potentilla, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as cinquefoils. The classification of Potentilla tabernaemontani longifrons can be broken down as follows: kingdom Plantae, class Magnoliopsida, order Rosales, family Rosaceae, genus Potentilla, species P. tabernaemontani, and subspecies P. t. longifrons. This complex classification reflects the plant's unique characteristics and its place within the broader plant kingdom.

intro: Potentilla tabernaemontani longifrons, commonly known as Long-leaved Silver Buffaloberry, is a shrub plant species belonging to the Rosaceae family. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Chinese medicine, where it is used to treat various health issues such as fever, cough, and respiratory problems, due to its purported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The bioactive constituents of Potentilla tabernaemontani longifrons include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Long-leaved Silver Buffaloberry is typically used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are among the most common herbal preparations made from this plant.
